title: Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan

seo.title: Authentic Dan Dan Noodles Recipe: Spicy Sichuan Delight
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan with our easy-to-follow recipe! Experience the fiery, savory, and numbing flavors of this classic Chinese dish at home.
longTailKeywords: how to make Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan
easy Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan recipe
authentic Chinese Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan
Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan step by step
best Dan Dan Noodles at home

seo.h1: Authentic Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan Recipe
h2Headings: Pro Tips for Perfect Dan Dan Noodles
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan with chili oil, minced pork, and bok choy.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the bold and complex flavors of Sichuan with this homemade Dan Dan Noodles recipe, featuring spicy chili oil and savory pork.

description: Dan Dan Noodles are a classic Sichuan street food, renowned for their explosive flavor profile. This dish features chewy noodles tossed in a rich, spicy, and savory sauce, topped with crispy minced pork and often some blanched greens. The perfect balance of chili oil, Sichuan peppercorns, sesame paste, and soy sauce creates an unforgettable culinary experience that will transport your taste buds to the heart of China.

ingredients.item: Bok choy or other green leafy vegetables

instructions.title: Prepare the Pork Topping
instructions.text: Heat vegetable oil in a wok or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the ground pork and stir-fry until it browns and crumbles. Drain any excess fat. Add minced ginger and garlic, stir-fry for 1 minute until fragrant. Deglaze with Shaoxing wine, then add soy sauce and the finely chopped Sichuan preserved mustard greens. Continue to stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until the pork is well coated and slightly crispy. Set aside.

instructions.title: Assemble the Sauce Base
instructions.text: For each serving bowl, combine the Sichuan chili oil, Chinkiang vinegar, light soy sauce, Chinese sesame paste, Sichuan peppercorn powder, sugar, and minced garlic. Stir well to ensure the sesame paste is fully incorporated and the sauce is emulsified. This base should be ready before the noodles are cooked.

instructions.title: Cook the Noodles
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Add the fresh Chinese wheat noodles and cook according to package instructions until al dente. If using thin spaghetti, cook until just tender.

instructions.title: Blanch Vegetables (Optional)
instructions.text: While the noodles are cooking, if using, add the bok choy or other green leafy vegetables to the boiling water during the last minute of noodle cooking, or blanch them separately until just tender-crisp. Remove vegetables and set aside.

instructions.title: Assemble the Dan Dan Noodles
instructions.text: Once the noodles are cooked, use a spider or tongs to transfer them directly from the boiling water into the prepared serving bowls with the sauce base. Do not drain them completely; a little noodle water helps emulsify the sauce. Toss the noodles vigorously with the sauce until every strand is coated.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Top each bowl of noodles with a generous spoonful of the prepared crispy pork topping. Arrange the blanched bok choy (if using) around the noodles. Garnish with chopped spring onions and crushed roasted peanuts. Serve immediately and enjoy the authentic flavors of Sichuan.

tips: For the best flavor, use freshly ground Sichuan peppercorns. Toast them lightly in a dry pan before grinding to release their full aroma and numbing sensation.

Authentic Chinese sesame paste is crucial; it's darker and more robust than Middle Eastern tahini. If unavailable, a mix of tahini and a little peanut butter can be a substitute, but the flavor will differ.
Serve Dan Dan Noodles immediately after assembly. The noodles can become soggy if left to sit, and the sauce is best enjoyed fresh and warm.
article: Dan Dan Noodles Sichuan is a beloved Chinese Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.

1 tbsp per bowl Light soy sauce
1 tbsp per bowl Chinese sesame paste
1/2 tsp per bowl Sichuan peppercorn powder
200g Bok choy or other green leafy vegetables
